steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The assertion claims Henry was motivated by “perceived financial opportunities.” To support this, we need evidence showing Henry assessed the financial worth of monastery properties before seizing them.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C — "In 1535, the year before enacting the Dissolution of the Monasteries, Henry commissioned a survey of the value of church holdings in England—the work, performed by sheriffs, bishops, and magistrates, began that January and was swiftly completed by the summer." reveals that Henry commissioned a survey in January 1535 to determine the value of church holdings, right before the Dissolution began—direct evidence he perceived financial opportunities in these properties.

The other choices describe demographics, what happened to monastery contents, and social impacts respectively—none address Henry's financial motivation.

The survey evidence is the correct support.
